title: amoora timorensis
intro: Amoora timorensis, also known as the Timor bengkoang or Timor jack, is a species of plant in the family Anacardiaceae, native to Timor Island in Indonesia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are derived from its sap and bark. The health benefits of Amoora timorensis include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a natural antiseptic and insecticide. The plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its sap is also used in traditional medicine to create herbal preparations, such as tinctures and ointments.
